results.possibleIdentity: John Collins was an electrical engineer and technical researcher at 3M (Minnesota Mining and Manufacturing Company) based in Saint Paul and Lake Elmo, Minnesota, during the 1960s and 1970s. He was a participant in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) program, a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between the artistic and scientific communities. As an engineer registered with E.A.T., he provided technical expertise in a wide range of fields including electrical engineering, physics, and computer systems. In the late 1970s, he was involved in developing systems implementation languages for microprocessor-based systems at 3M, specifically utilizing PASCAL on PDP-11 hosts for system monitoring. His technical profile included expertise in optics (lasers, holography, and fiber optics), acoustics (audio systems and sound generation), and advanced electronics (integrated circuits, transistors, and control theory).
